assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Case A creates two tasks for stream rhtpa-2.2. Task 1 (upstream backport) has labels: 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123' and link: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, type: "Depend"). Task 2 (downstream propagation) has the same labels and includes link: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Depend"). Both tasks match the required standard labels and 'Depend' link type to TC-8020.

assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: In cross-stream.md: 'Stream rhtpa-2.1: **No CVE Jira exists**' and 'Since stream rhtpa-2.1 is affected but has no CVE Jira, preemptive remediation tasks are created per Step 7 Case B.' In remediation.md: 'Cross-stream impact analysis shows stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected (tokio 1.40.0 < 1.42.0). A JQL search for sibling CVE Jiras with label CVE-2026-55123 returns no results for stream rhtpa-2.1 -- no CVE Jira exists for that stream. Per Step 7 Case B, preemptive remediation tasks are created.' Two preemptive tasks (Task 3 and Task 4) are created for stream rhtpa-2.1.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Task 3 (preemptive upstream backport for rhtpa-2.1) has labels: 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive' (line 147). Task 4 (preemptive downstream propagation for rhtpa-2.1) has labels: 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive' (line 216). Both Jira API calls also confirm these exact labels: labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"].

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Task 3 link: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-upstream-task-key>, type: "Related") (line 210). Task 4 link: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-downstream-task-key>, type: "Related") (line 274). In cross-stream.md: 'Link type: Uses "Related" (not "Depend") to TC-8020, because TC-8020 belongs to a different stream (rhtpa-2.2)' (line 48). All preemptive task links use 'Related', not 'Depend'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Task 3 description begins with: '**Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' (lines 151-154). Task 4 description begins with the identical prefix: '**Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' (lines 221-224). Both preemptive tasks include the prefix noting TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating CVE.
